theory.canonical_statement: The rotating double helix describes an optical propagation medium combining spatial helical paths with temporal rotation.
theory.technical_interpretation: The rotating double helix describes an optical propagation medium combining spatial helical paths with temporal rotation.

canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[
s_1(t,\Omega)=R_z(\Omega t)\!\left(R\cos\!\frac{2\pi t}{P},\,R\sin\!\frac{2\pi t}{P},\,t\right)
\]
\[
s_2(t,\Omega)=R_z(\Omega t)\!\left(R\cos\!\left(\frac{2\pi t}{P}+\pi\right),\,R\sin\!\left(\frac{2\pi t}{P}+\pi\right),\,t\right)
\]

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This fixes the first rotating helical strand as an explicit parametric carrier.
This adds the antipodal strand that completes the double-helix geometry.
This closes the entry by defining the rotation operator that transports both strands.
